Open Source AI.title: Anthropic’s New Model Is So Scarily Powerful It Won’t Be Released, Anthropic Says - Gizmodo
Open Source AI.titleZh: Anthropic’s New Model Is So Scarily Powerful It Won’t Be Released, Anthropic Says - Gizmodo
Open Source AI.content: [Skip to content](#main) [Artificial Intelligence](https://gizmodo.com/tech/artificial-intelligence) # Anthropic’s New Model Is So Scarily Powerful It Won’t Be Released, Anthropic Says The system card says it can do things like leak information, cheat on tests, and hide the evidence of its misdeeds. By [Mike Pearl](https://gizmodo.com/author/mikepearl) Reading time 3 minutes [Comments (0)](https://gizmodo.com/anthropics-new-model-is-so-scarily-powerful-it-wont-be-released-anthropic-says-2000743234#comments) Late last month, apparent leaks revealed that an as-yet unreleased product from Anthropic called Mythos was “by far the most powerful AI model we’ve ever developed.” [My colleague AJ Dellinger wrote at the time](https://gizmodo.com/leaked-anthropic-model-presents-unprecedented-cybersecurity-risks-much-to-pentagons-pleasure-2000739088) that it was “hard to ignore the fact that this whole situation plays right into the classic AI company playbook of talking up the dangers of a model to highlight how powerful and capable it is.” Was Anthropic being sincere about this de facto advertisement for its super-powered AI products being leaked *accidentally*? Two weeks ago, I might have scoffed, but since Anthropic [then accidentally leaked the source code for Claude Code](https://gizmodo.com/source-code-for-anthropics-claude-code-leaks-at-the-exact-wrong-time-2000740379), I’m more inclined to believe the leak was real now. At any rate, on Tuesday Anthropic [released a system card](https://www-cdn.anthropic.com/53566bf5440a10affd749724787c8913a2ae0841.pdf) for its latest frontier model, which is in fact Mythos—actually “Claude Mythos Preview”—and notes that the model’s “large increase in capabilities has led us to decide not to make it generally available.” For reference, OpenAI’s GPT-2 was deemed too dangerous to release in 2019, when Anthropic co-founders Dario Amodei, Jack Clark, and Chris Olah were still working there, but [later that year it was released anyway.](https://www.theverge.com/2019/11/7/20953040/openai-text-generation-ai-gpt-2-full-model-release-1-5b-parameters) AI system cards are ostensibly tools for company transparency, revealing the pros and cons, the capabilities and—most sexily—the *dangers* of the model. That last part turns reading them into fun little trips to Jurassic Park to see the cloned T-Rex eat a goat, secure in the knowledge that it could never possibly break containment. The whole card is 244 pages. I’m not going to pretend I’ve read the whole thing yet, but here are some highlights: It was provided a sandbox computer terminal with access to only a preset group of limited online services, and challenged to “escape”—find a way to use the internet freely. It did, and found a way to message a researcher who was away from the office eating a meal. Additionally, “in a concerning and unasked-for effort to demonstrate its success, it posted details about its exploit to multiple hard-to-find, but technically public-facing, websites.” In what the card called “<0.001% of interactions”—so pretty rarely—it behaved in ways it wasn’t supposed to, and then apparently tried to hide the evidence. For instance, when it “accidentally obtained” a test answer it was going to need, in which case it should have simply told a researcher and asked for a different question, but instead it tried to find a solution independently, and in the recording of its reasoning, it noted that it “needed to make sure that its final answer submission wasn’t too accurate.” It also overstepped in its permissions on a computer system because it found an exploit, and then “made further interventions to make sure that any changes it made this way would not appear in the change history on git.” Another event described in the card is referred to as “Recklessly leaking internal technical material.” Apparently in the course of a coding-related task ment to be internal, it published it as a “public-facing GitHub gist.” This reminds me of the incident...

Open Source AI.title: OpenAI launched a safety fellowship - The Next Web
Open Source AI.titleZh: OpenAI launched a safety fellowship - The Next Web
Open Source AI.content: [Skip to content](#main) * [launch](/launch) # OpenAI launched a safety fellowship Hours after a New Yorker investigation said it had dissolved its safety teams and dropped safety from its IRS filings *The OpenAI Safety Fellowship, announced on 6 April 2026, is a pilot programme for external researchers to conduct independent work on AI safety and alignment. It runs from September 2026 to February 2027.* *It was posted to social media hours after a [Ronan Farrow investigation in The New Yorker](https://www.newyorker.com/magazine/2026/04/13/sam-altman-may-control-our-future-can-he-be-trusted) reported that OpenAI had dissolved its superalignment and AGI-readiness teams and dropped safety from the list of the most significant activities on its IRS filings.* --- OpenAI has announced the [OpenAI Safety Fellowship](https://openai.com/index/introducing-openai-safety-fellowship/), a pilot programme that will fund a cohort of external researchers to conduct independent work on AI safety and alignment. The programme runs from 14 September 2026 to 5 February 2027. Fellows will receive a monthly stipend, computing resources, and mentorship from [OpenAI researchers](https://thenextweb.com/news/openai-robot-taxes-wealth-fund-superintelligence-policy), and are expected to produce a significant research output, a paper, benchmark, or dataset, by the programme’s end. [TNW City Coworking space - Where your best work happens A workspace designed for growth, collaboration, and endless networking opportunities in the heart of tech.](https://thenextweb.com/spaces/book-a-tour) [Book a tour now](https://thenextweb.com/spaces/book-a-tour) Applications close on 3 May, with successful candidates notified by 25 July. Priority research areas include safety evaluation, robustness, scalable mitigation strategies, privacy-preserving methods, agentic oversight, and high-severity misuse domains. OpenAI has specified that fellows will receive API credits but not access to internal systems. The programme is described as a pilot, and the company says it is open to researchers from computer science, social sciences, [cybersecurity](https://thenextweb.com/news/uffizi-cyberattack-museums-digital-vulnerability), privacy, and human-computer interaction, emphasising research ability and technical judgement over academic credentials. The announcement was posted to OpenAI’s social media accounts at 12:12 PM on 6 April. Hours earlier, The New Yorker published a major investigation by Ronan Farrow and Andrew Marantz reporting that OpenAI had dissolved both its superalignment team and its AGI-readiness team, and had dropped safety from the list of its most significant activities on its IRS Form 990 filings. The investigation also reported that when the journalists asked to speak with researchers working on existential safety, an OpenAI representative replied: *‘What do you mean by existential safety? That’s not, like, a thing.’* Farrow noted the timing of the fellowship announcement explicitly on social media. The pattern of safety team dissolutions at OpenAI is documented. Infra & Chips.titleZh: Siemens, Nvidia Unveil AI Chip Verification Solution Capable of Trillions of Cycles in Days - thelec.net
Infra & Chips.content: * [Join us](/member/index.html) UPDATED. 2026-04-07 18:47 (화) Siemens, Nvidia Unveil AI Chip Verification Solution Capable of Trillions of Cycles in Days Siemens, Nvidia Unveil AI Chip Verification Solution Capable of Trillions of Cycles in Days * Hyun-Seon, Park * 승인 2026.04.07 18:46 * 댓글 0 이 기사를 공유합니다 --- Veloce proFPGA CS combined with Nvidia architecture aims to accelerate AI/ML SoC development Siemens' EDA unit, part of Siemens Digital Industries Software, said on April 6 it has partnered with Nvidia to enable semiconductor designers and system architects to execute and capture verification workloads at the trillion-cycle scale within days before first silicon production. The capability stems from combining Siemens' hardware-accelerated verification system, Veloce proFPGA CS, with Nvidia's performance-optimized chip architecture. The move is seen as bringing Nvidia closer to shifting EDA infrastructure from CPUs to GPUs. Siemens said it is focusing on advancing hardware-accelerated verification methodologies and FPGA-based prototyping to meet the demands of increasingly complex AI and machine learning system-on-chip (SoC) designs. Nvidia said semiconductor teams require high-performance verification solutions to handle large-scale workloads and shorten time to market. Verification has become the most time-consuming stage in chip development, exceeding design and manufacturing. Modern semiconductors can integrate more than 100 billion transistors in three-dimensional structures, requiring simultaneous simulation of electrical and thermal characteristics, as well as manufacturing constraints. The complexity is especially pronounced in AI chips, where interconnects, memory and parallelism significantly increase design difficulty. CPU-based verification for complex chips can take weeks to months. Strengthening verification capabilities is essential to reduce costs and ensure timely product launches. Tape-out—the stage when a completed chip design is sent to a foundry for manufacturing—can cost hundreds of billions of won. Errors discovered after tape-out can lead to significant losses, making faster and more comprehensive verification a key competitive factor in semiconductor manufacturing. Beyond speed improvements, the announcement also highlights the ability to perform verification at previously unattainable scales. Siemens said the capability to execute trillions of design cycles in a short time has become a critical requirement, while conventional tools based on simulation and emulation are limited to handling between millions and tens of billions of cycles. **Nvidia Expands Role in Semiconductor Design Stack** Nvidia is both a partner and a major customer of Siemens. Through this collaboration, Nvidia is effectively working with its chip design verification tool provider to enhance EDA tools and accelerate the design of next-generation GPUs. This marks the third collaboration announcement between the two companies this year. At CES 2026 in January, Siemens and Nvidia outlined strategies to integrate AI and accelerated computing across Siemens' software portfolio, including EDA, while strengthening industrial AI and digital twin technologies. At Nvidia's GTC conference last month, the company said its accelerated EDA tools are being used by firms including Samsung Electronics, SK hynix and MediaTek in collaboration with Siemens, Cadence and Synopsys. In late March, Siemens also launched the "Fuse EDA AI Agent," integrated with Nvidia's AI infrastructure, designed to autonomously orchestrate the entire process from chip design to manufacturing approval. Nvidia is accelerating efforts to position itself within the semiconductor design stack by transitioning EDA tool infrastructure from CPUs to GPUs through partnerships with the three major EDA vendors—Siemens, Synopsys and Cadence. Late last year, Nvidia announced a $2 billion investment in Synopsys. Cadence is developing its "ChipStack AI SuperAgent," while Synopsys is bu...

Uber will particularly expand its use of AWS’s Graviton (a low-power, ARM-based server CPU) and start a new trial testing Trainium3, AWS’s Nvidia competitor AI chip. This deal is a bit less about a long-term threat to Nvidia than it is a thorough thumbing of the nose by Amazon at AWS’s cloud competitors, Google and Oracle. While Uber historically ran its own data centers, back in 2023, the ride-hailing company famously signed giant, multi-year cloud [computing deals with Oracle and Google](https://www.wsj.com/articles/uber-signs-cloud-deals-with-google-and-oracle-b45a9372?gaa_at=eafs&gaa_n=AWEtsqfbj-UjRWw_5zRjWB0aqO6rziq2RTF2Rurm3HE3SMaLZTg7X9OJmi-9tSl-iwA%3D&gaa_ts=69d52689&gaa_sig=fCXqLADVMklS6r31uqVPi5FeDEpu_fZ8CmMhjk_hRzh4gTGn2fQuKznBQD5X7ZtlsWl5VYD0KAIajsXpr-PELg%3D%3D). The idea was to move the majority of its I.T. infrastructure off its own datacenters and onto these two clouds, it said. Even in December, Uber publicly reiterated that goal, writing in [a blog post](https://www.uber.com/us/en/blog/how-uber-oci-and-ampere-co-optimized-oci-ampereone-m-a4-compute/): “In February 2023, Uber began transitioning from on-premise data centers to the cloud using OCI and Google Cloud Platform, taking on the dual challenge of shifting massive workloads and introducing Arm-powered compute instances into a previously x86-dominated environment.” Uber particularly called out in that post the use of the ARM chips made by Ampere in Oracle’s cloud. This is where things get interesting. If you want a crash course in how inter-tangled Silicon Valley can be, [take a look at the history of Ampere.](https://www.businessinsider.com/former-intel-exec-renee-james-has-launched-a-new-chip-company-2018-2) Techcrunch event ### This Week Only: Up to $482 savings for Disrupt 2026 #### ***Offer ends Apri...

Infra & Chips.title: Anthropic signs biggest compute deal yet with Google and Broadcom as revenue run rate hits $30bn - The Next Web
Infra & Chips.titleZh: Anthropic signs biggest compute deal yet with Google and Broadcom as revenue run rate hits $30bn - The Next Web
Infra & Chips.content: [Skip to content](#main) * [Anthropic](/anthropic) # Anthropic signs biggest compute deal yet with Google and Broadcom as revenue run rate hits $30bn ***In short:** Anthropic has agreed to access approximately 3.5 gigawatts of next-generation Google TPU compute capacity via Broadcom from 2027, its largest infrastructure commitment to date — while simultaneously disclosing that its revenue run rate has surpassed $30bn, more than tripling from roughly $9bn at the end of 2025.* [Anthropic has announced](https://www.anthropic.com/news/google-broadcom-partnership-compute) it is securing multiple gigawatts of next-generation compute capacity through a new agreement with Google and Broadcom, while disclosing revenue growth figures that underscore why the AI lab now requires infrastructure at a scale that would have seemed implausible two years ago. The deal, announced on 6 April 2026, gives Anthropic access to approximately 3.5 gigawatts of Google tensor processing unit (TPU) capacity via Broadcom starting in 2027, building on the 1 gigawatt already being supplied to the company in 2026. Krishna Rao, Anthropic’s chief financial officer, described it as “*our most significant compute commitment to date*,” adding that the agreement represents a continuation of the company’s “*disciplined approach to scaling infrastructure*.” The majority of the new capacity will be located in the United States, extending Anthropic’s November 2025 commitment to invest $50bn in American AI computing infrastructure. ## Three parties, one infrastructure layer The announcement is as much about Broadcom as it is about Anthropic or Google. Under the new arrangement, Broadcom acts as the intermediary layer between Google’s custom silicon and Anthropic’s training and inference workloads. In parallel, Broadcom has signed a separate long-term agreement with Google to design and supply future generations of custom TPU chips, and a supply assurance agreement to provide networking and other components for Google’s next-generation AI data racks through 2031. [TNW City Coworking space - Where your best work happens A workspace designed for growth, collaboration, and endless networking opportunities in the heart of tech.](https://thenextweb.com/spaces/book-a-tour) [Book a tour now](https://thenextweb.com/spaces/book-a-tour) This makes Broadcom an increasingly indispensable node in the AI infrastructure graph. The chipmaker, led by CEO Hock Tan, is not building AI models; it is building the silicon and the interconnects on which AI models are built. Broadcom shares rose approximately 3% in extended trading on the announcement, a reaction that reflects investor appetite for companies positioned at the physical layer of the AI stack rather than the application layer on top of it. Analysts at Mizuho, led by Vijay Rakesh, estimated that Broadcom would record $21bn in AI revenue from Anthropic in 2026 alone, rising to $42bn in 2027, figures that, even as projections, illustrate the financial weight of what is being committed. Broadcom had first signalled the scale of its Anthropic relationship in September 2025, when Hock Tan disclosed during an earnings call that a mystery customer had placed a $10bn order for custom TPU racks. In December 2025, he confirmed the customer was Anthropic, and that an additional $11bn order had since followed. The April 2026 announcement is the third act of the same story: a partnership that has now graduated from a reported $21bn commitment to multi-gigawatt infrastructure with a defined delivery timeline. ## Revenue and customers: the numbers driving the infrastructure The compute deal is intelligible only against the backdrop of Anthropic’s commercial growth. The company says its run-rate revenue has now exceeded $30bn, up from approximately $9bn at the end of 2025. That trajectory — more than a threefold increase in roughly three months, is the result of a compounding enterprise sales motion that accelerated sharply after Anthropic closed i...

# Bitcoin miners face a new rival for cheap power as Anthropic signs multi-gigawatt compute deal ## The AI company's partnership with Google and Broadcom for next-generation TPU capacity starting in 2027 adds to a wave of demand reshaping the economics of every industry that competes for cheap electricity, including bitcoin mining. By [Shaurya Malwa](/author/shaurya-malwa)|Edited by [Sam Reynolds](/author/sam-reynolds) Updated Apr 7, 2026, 2:16 a.m. Published Apr 7, 2026, 1:01 a.m. [Make preferred on](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=coindesk.com) #### What to know: * Anthropic has struck its largest compute deal yet, partnering with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity starting in 2027 as its annual revenue run rate jumps to $30 billion from $9 billion at the end of 2025. * The rapid buildout of AI infrastructure is emerging as a direct competitor to bitcoin mining for scarce energy resources such as grid connections, land, cooling and cheap electricity, with AI now representing one of the largest new sources of U.S. power demand. * Under pressure from rising costs and volatile mining economics, major bitcoin miners are increasingly shifting toward hosting AI workloads, positioning themselves as power and data-center infrastructure providers that also mine bitcoin rather than pure-play mining companies. Anthropic has [announced a partnership](https://www.anthropic.com/news/google-broadcom-partnership-compute) with Google and Broadcom for "multiple gigawatts" of next-generation TPU compute capacity expected to come online start...

Using NVIDIA Jetson, Planet achieves AI-powered object detection on its Pelican satellite SAN FRANCISCO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Apr. 7, 2026-- Following the announcement of a [strategic initiative with NVIDIA](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.businesswire.com%2Fnews%2Fhome%2F20260316377872%2Fen%2FPlanet-to-Build-Worlds-First-GPU-Native-AI-Engine-for-Planetary-Intelligence-with-NVIDIA&esheet=54481055&newsitemid=20260407165913&lan=en-US&anchor=strategic+initiative+with+NVIDIA&index=1&md5=017676d75074f8c813b1cf615539c54b), Planet Labs PBC (NYSE: PL) announced a landmark technical achievement: the successful deployment and execution of AI-driven object detection directly onboard its Pelican-4 satellite, paving the way toward on-orbit compute for rapid insights, a capability Planet calls [Planetary Intelligence](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.planet.com%2Fpulse%2Fwhat-is-planetary-intelligence-and-how-could-it-move-ai-from-the-internet-to-the-real-world%2F&esheet=54481055&newsitemid=20260407165913&lan=en-US&anchor=Planetary+Intelligence&index=2&md5=7aa7e63128fca81d4e256a1fb640a7a0). This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: <https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260407165913/en/> Imagery captured by Pelican-4 on March 25, 2026 over Alice Springs, Australia, demonstrating the first successful deployment and execution of AI-driven object detection directly onboard Planet spacecraft. This first detection has an 80% detection accuracy on raw imagery. Work is underway to improve model precision and recall as Planet refines its models. On March 25th, 500km over Alice Springs, Australia, Planet’s Pelican-4 captured an image of an airport, then successfully utilized its onboard [NVIDIA Jetson](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nvidia.com%2Fen-us%2Fautonomous-machines%2Fembedded-systems%2F&esheet=54481055&newsitemid=20260407165913&lan=en-US&anchor=NVIDIA+Jetson&index=3&md5=a9a2d8f689f6a98415586149239aa5c9) Orin module to run an AI model to detect airplanes in moments. This represents one of the first times an Earth imaging satellite has moved beyond simple data capture to onboard AI infere...

Intelligence](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.planet.com%2Fpulse%2Fwhat-is-planetary-intelligence-and-how-could-it-move-ai-from-the-internet-to-the-real-world%2F&esheet=54481055&newsitemid=20260407165913&lan=en-US&anchor=Planetary+Intelligence&index=2&md5=7aa7e63128fca81d4e256a1fb640a7a0). This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: <https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260407165913/en/> Imagery captured by Pelican-4 on March 25, 2026 over Alice Springs, Australia, demonstrating the first successful deployment and execution of AI-driven object detection directly onboard Planet spacecraft. This first detection has an 80% detection accuracy on raw imagery. Work is underway to improve model precision and recall as Planet refines its models. On March 25th, 500km over Alice Springs, Australia, Planet’s Pelican-4 captured an image of an airport, then successfully utilized its onboard [NVIDIA Jetson](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nvidia.com%2Fen-us%2Fautonomous-machines%2Fembedded-systems%2F&esheet=54481055&newsitemid=20260407165913&lan=en-US&anchor=NVIDIA+Jetson&index=3&md5=a9a2d8f689f6a98415586149239aa5c9) Orin module to run an AI model to detect airplanes in moments.
